thought.text: I will create the `time_slots.yaml` blueprint file. To resolve the `DATA_MODEL_UX_MISMATCH` gap, we explicitly add `organization_id` to `time_slots` and configure `belongs_to_organization: true`. This ensures robust multi-tenant scoping. Staff can manage slots but cannot delete them, and customers can only read them.

action.file_text:
model: TimeSlot
slug: time_slots
table: time_slots

options:
  belongs_to_organization: true
  soft_deletes: false
  audit_trail: false
  pagination: true
  per_page: 25

columns:
  service_id:
    type: foreignId
    foreign_model: Service
    nullable: false
    index: true
  staff_member_id:
    type: foreignId
    foreign_model: StaffMember
    nullable: true
    index: true
  starts_at:
    type: datetime
    nullable: false
  ends_at:
    type: datetime
    nullable: false
  available:
    type: boolean
    default: true
    nullable: false
    filterable: true

relationships:
  - type: belongsTo
    model: Organization
  - type: belongsTo
    model: Service
  - type: belongsTo
    model: StaffMember
  - type: hasOne
    model: Booking

permissions:
  owner:
    actions: [index, show, store, update, destroy]
    show_fields: "*"
    create_fields: "*"
    update_fields: "*"
  admin:
    actions: [index, show, store, update, destroy]
    show_fields: "*"
    create_fields: "*"
    update_fields: "*"
  staff:
    actions: [index, show, store, update]
    show_fields: "*"
    create_fields: "*"
    update_fields: "*"
  customer:
    actions: [index, show]
    show_fields:
      - id
      - service_id
      - staff_member_id
      - starts_at
      - ends_at
      - available
    create_fields: []
    update_fields: []

tool_call.name: guarded_file_editor
tool_call.arguments:
